title: rumex bucephalophorus subsp. gallicus
intro: Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. gallicus, also known as French dock or bitter dock, is a perennial herb in the buckwheat family, characterized by its reddish-brown stems and leaves with a distinctive bitter taste. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. gallicus include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce blood pressure, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
